description: Concurrency limits and scheduling constraints for the multi-model orchestration pipeline.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of tasks that may execute concurrently within a single run. Tasks beyond this limit are queued in deterministic topological order with lexicographic tie-breaking.

retry.description: Maximum number of execution attempts for a single task across all failure classes. Exceeding this limit transitions the task to 'failed' and the run to FAILED_TERMINAL if no other tasks can proceed.

replanning.description: Maximum number of REPLAN_REQUIRED transitions permitted for a single run. Exceeding this limit transitions the run to FAILED_TERMINAL.

timeout_per_phase.description: Maximum wall-clock time allowed for each phase. Exceeding a phase timeout triggers an infrastructure failure and retries the phase without replanning, up to max_attempts_per_task.

scheduling_constraints.description: Two tasks may only run concurrently if their dependency subgraphs are disjoint. A task's dependency subgraph includes all transitive predecessors.
Two tasks may only run concurrently if their declared write scopes have no overlapping paths. Overlap detection is performed by scope_conflict.js.
When multiple tasks are eligible for scheduling simultaneously, they are ordered lexicographically by task ID. This tie-breaking rule is applied at every scheduling decision point.
A workspace path may only be written by one task at a time. The scheduler must acquire a path-level lock before a task's write phase begins and release it after the write is committed or rolled back.
A task's completion eligibility is evaluated atomically only after its verification.json entry is fully written and fsync'd. No task may transition to 'completed' before this condition is met.
